marketplace.author: egoist
marketplace.description: Fetches URLs and YouTube video transcripts through an MCP server.
marketplace.about: Fetch allows users to retrieve content from URLs, extracting either HTML or Markdown, and also provides the functionality to fetch YouTube video transcripts, all through a streamlined MCP server interface.
key_features: Extracts transcripts from YouTube videos.
Supports fetching content via MCP protocol
Offers both stdio and SSE server options.
Fetches URLs and returns HTML or Markdown content.
use_cases: Programmatically accessing and processing website content.
Integrating URL and transcript retrieval into LLM chatbots.
Automating the extraction of transcripts for research or analysis.

marketplace.author: cyberchitta
marketplace.description: Retrieves text and HTML content from bot-protected websites for AI assistant consumption.
marketplace.about: Scrapling Fetch empowers AI assistants to access and understand content from websites employing anti-automation measures. By acting as a bridge between AI and bot-protected sites, it enables low-volume retrieval of documentation, reference materials, and other textual content.
key_features: Offers different protection levels (basic, stealth, max-stealth) for varied website security.
Enables content extraction using regular expressions.
Bypasses anti-automation measures on websites.
Provides tools for fetching complete web pages with pagination.
Returns content position information for follow-up queries.
use_cases: Providing AI assistants with up-to-date documentation from protected sites.
Extracting specific information from websites employing anti-automation measures.
Enabling AI to access reference materials behind bot detection.

tools.name: s_fetch_page
tools.description: Fetches a complete web page with pagination support. Retrieves content from websites with bot-detection avoidance. For best performance, start with 'basic' mode (fastest), then only escalate to 'stealth' or 'max-stealth' modes if basic mode fails.

tools.name: s_fetch_pattern
tools.description: Extracts content matching regex patterns from web pages. Retrieves specific content from websites with bot-detection avoidance.

marketplace.author: sylphxltd
marketplace.description: Securely reads and extracts text, metadata, and page counts from PDF files (local or URL) for use by AI agents.
marketplace.about: Empower AI agents to securely read and extract information from PDF files using this Model Content Protocol (MCP) server. It allows for extracting full text, specific pages, metadata, and page counts from both local files and public URLs.
key_features: Extract full text, specific pages, metadata, and page count
Easy integration via npm or Docker
Handles both local relative paths and public URLs
Secure PDF parsing within a confined project context
Structured JSON output for easy agent parsing
use_cases: Building knowledge bases from PDF content
Automated extraction of metadata from PDF documents
Enabling AI agents to process information from PDF reports

marketplace.author: Wildebeest
marketplace.description: Manipulate PDF forms by finding PDF files, extracting form field information, and visualizing form fields.
marketplace.about: This toolkit provides capabilities for interacting with PDF forms, leveraging the Machine Conversation Protocol (MCP) and PyMuPDF. It allows users to locate PDF files across multiple directories, extract detailed form field information, and visually highlight form fields within the PDF documents.
key_features: Find PDF files across multiple directories
Extract form field information from PDF files
Visualize form fields in PDF documents
use_cases: Quickly locate all PDF files in specified folders or projects.
Analyze form structure and understand the fields available for filling.
Visually inspect the layout and position of form fields in a document.

key_features: Returns structured search results with titles, URLs, and descriptions
Multi-engine web search (Bing, Baidu, DuckDuckGo, Brave, Exa, CSDN)
No API keys or authentication required
Support for fetching individual article content (e.g., CSDN)
Configurable HTTP proxy for accessing restricted resources

Performing web searches without relying on paid API keys or authentication
Programmatically fetching full article content from supported websites like CSDN

marketplace.author: domdomegg
marketplace.description: Enables reading, creating, and editing files on the local filesystem through operations like view, create, string replacement, and line insertion.
key_features: View file contents with line numbers or list directory contents
Create or overwrite files with content
Exact string replacement in files
Line-based text insertion
use_cases: Simple file viewing and editing for AI assistants
Creating new files programmatically
Making targeted string replacements in code
marketplace.about: A lightweight MCP server for filesystem operations including reading, creating, and editing files. It provides four focused tools — view for reading file contents or listing directories with line numbers, create for writing new files, str_replace for making precise string replacements, and insert for adding text at specific line positions. Designed for simplicity and ease of integration with AI assistants for tasks like config updates, code refactoring, and report generation.

marketplace.author: jae-jae
marketplace.description: An MCP server that retrieves web page content using Playwright headless browser, capable of extracting main content and converting to Markdown format.
key_features: Uses Playwright headless browser to parse JavaScript
Supports intelligent extraction of main content and conversion to Markdown
Batch URL fetching support
Configurable timeouts and navigation options
use_cases: Scraping JavaScript-rendered web pages for AI consumption
Converting web content to clean Markdown for processing
Batch fetching multiple URLs for research or analysis
marketplace.about: An MCP server for fetching web page content using a Playwright headless browser. Unlike traditional web scrapers, it executes JavaScript to handle dynamic web content and modern web applications. Features an intelligent Readability algorithm that automatically extracts the main content from web pages, removing ads, navigation, and other non-essential elements. Supports both HTML and Markdown output formats, and enables concurrent fetching of multiple URLs for parallel processing.

marketplace.author: zcaceres
marketplace.description: Provides functionality to fetch web content in various formats, including HTML, JSON, plain text, and Markdown.
key_features: Fetch web content as HTML, JSON, plain text, or Markdown
Extract article content with Mozilla Readability (strips ads, nav, boilerplate)
Extract YouTube video transcripts
Proxy support for all requests
use_cases: Fetching clean article content stripped of ads and navigation
Extracting YouTube transcripts for summarization
Programmatically accessing web content in multiple formats
marketplace.about: An MCP server for fetching web content in multiple formats — HTML, JSON, plain text, Markdown, readable article content, and YouTube transcripts. All tools accept common parameters including custom headers, maximum content length, start index for pagination, and proxy support. The fetch_readable tool uses Mozilla Readability to extract the main article content, stripping navigation, ads, and boilerplate for clean output.

marketplace.description: MCP server for fetching web content and processing images. Extracts article body as Markdown and page images with optimization.
key_features: Web content extraction with automatic Markdown formatting
Article title extraction
Image processing with base64 return, file save, cross-origin support
Configurable image count, size, and quality
use_cases: Fetching web pages with embedded images for multimodal AI
Extracting and optimizing page images for analysis
Content scraping with image preservation
marketplace.about: A Model Context Protocol server for fetching web content and processing images. It automatically extracts and formats web content as Markdown, extracts article titles, and optionally processes images from web pages with optimization including base64 return, file saving, and cross-origin support. Images can be merged into a single JPEG or returned individually, with configurable count, size, and quality settings.

tools.name: pdf-resume
tools.description: Generate a professional resume PDF from JSON Resume format. Supports layout customization, date/locale formatting, styling, fonts, and automatic page breaks.

filename.description: Optional logical filename (metadata only). Storage uses UUID. Defaults to "resume.pdf".

resume.description: Resume data in JSON Resume format

font.description: Font for the PDF. Defaults to "auto" (system font detection). Built-ins are limited to ASCII; provide a path or URL for full Unicode.

background.description: Page background color (hex like "#000000" or named color). Default: white.

hyperlink.description: Color for hyperlink text (hex like "#0066CC" or named color). Default: #0066CC (classic browser blue).

pageSize.description: Page size preset (default: "LETTER"). Use "A4" for international standard.

sections.description: Sections configuration for section ordering and field templates

source.description: Path to data in resume schema using dot notation. Examples: "basics" (for header), "basics.summary", "work", "volunteer", "education", "awards", "certificates", "publications", "skills", "languages", "interests", "projects", "references", "meta.customField".

render.description: Built-in renderer type. Auto-inferred from data shape if omitted. Only needed when: (1) you want "header" rendering (never auto-inferred), or (2) you want to force a specific renderer. Not needed if using template.

template.description: LiquidJS template for custom rendering. Use instead of render for full control. Receives source data as template context (e.g., {{ name }}, {{ keywords | join: ", " }}).

showTenure.description: Show tenure duration for work/volunteer sections

fieldTemplates.description: LiquidJS templates for field-level rendering. Filters: date (format dates), default (fallback value), tenure (calculate duration), join (join arrays)

location.description: Location display template (default: "{{ city }}{% if region %}, {{ region }}{% endif %}")

dateRange.description: Date range template. Date format tokens: YYYY (4-digit year), YY (2-digit), MMMM (January), MMM (Jan), MM (01), M (1), DD (05), D (5). Default: "{{ start | date: 'MMM YYYY' }} – {{ end | date: 'MMM YYYY' | default: 'Present' }}"

degree.description: Education degree template (default: "{{ studyType }}{% if area %}, {{ area }}{% endif %}")

credential.description: Credential metadata template (default: "{{ title | default: name }}{% if awarder %}, {{ awarder }}{% endif %}")

language.description: Language display template (default: "{{ language }}{% if fluency %} ({{ fluency }}){% endif %}")

layout.description: Spatial arrangement of sections. Omit for single-column (default). Use style: "two-column" with columns config for sidebar layouts.

width.description: Column width as percentage ("30%") or points (150)

sections.description: Section sources to place in this column. Use source paths from section config: "basics", "basics.summary", "work", "volunteer", "education", "awards", "certificates", "publications", "skills", "languages", "interests", "projects", "references", "meta.customField". Sections not assigned to a column go to the right column by default.

Precise positioning using Yoga flexbox layout
Professional resume generation from JSON Resume format
PNG image generation from PDF pages
Text measurement before rendering

Building dashboards and precisely-positioned PDF layouts

tools.name: search_papers
tools.description:
Search for papers on arXiv with advanced filtering and query optimization.

QUERY CONSTRUCTION GUIDELINES:
- Use QUOTED PHRASES for exact matches: "multi-agent systems", "neural networks", "machine learning"
- Combine related concepts with OR: "AI agents" OR "software agents" OR "intelligent agents"  
- Use field-specific searches for precision:
  - ti:"exact title phrase" - search in titles only
  - au:"author name" - search by author
  - abs:"keyword" - search in abstracts only
- Use ANDNOT to exclude unwanted results: "machine learning" ANDNOT "survey"
- For best results, use 2-4 core concepts rather than long keyword lists

ADVANCED SEARCH PATTERNS:
- Field + phrase: ti:"transformer architecture" for papers with exact title phrase
- Multiple fields: au:"Smith" AND ti:"quantum" for author Smith's quantum papers  
- Exclusions: "deep learning" ANDNOT ("survey" OR "review") to exclude survey papers
- Broad + narrow: "artificial intelligence" AND (robotics OR "computer vision")

CATEGORY FILTERING (highly recommended for relevance):
Computer Science:
- cs.AI: Artificial Intelligence
- cs.LG: Machine Learning
- cs.CL: Computation and Language (NLP)
- cs.CV: Computer Vision
- cs.MA: Multi-Agent Systems
- cs.RO: Robotics
- cs.NE: Neural and Evolutionary Computing
- cs.IR: Information Retrieval
- cs.HC: Human-Computer Interaction
- cs.CR: Cryptography and Security
- cs.DB: Databases
Statistics & Math:
- stat.ML: Machine Learning (Statistics)
- stat.AP: Applications
- math.OC: Optimization and Control
- math.ST: Statistics Theory
Physics & Other:
- quant-ph: Quantum Physics
- eess.SP: Signal Processing
- eess.AS: Audio and Speech Processing
- physics.data-an: Data Analysis and Statistics

EXAMPLES OF EFFECTIVE QUERIES:
- ti:"reinforcement learning" with categories: ["cs.LG", "cs.AI"] - for RL papers by title
- au:"Hinton" AND "deep learning" with categories: ["cs.LG"] - for Hinton's deep learning work
- "multi-agent" ANDNOT "survey" with categories: ["cs.MA"] - exclude survey papers
- abs:"transformer" AND ti:"attention" with categories: ["cs.CL"] - attention papers with transformer abstracts

DATE FILTERING: Use YYYY-MM-DD format for historical research:
- date_to: "2015-12-31" - for foundational/classic work (pre-2016)
- date_from: "2020-01-01" - for recent developments (post-2020)
- Both together for specific time periods

RESULT QUALITY: Default sort is RELEVANCE (most pertinent results first). Use sort_by: "date" to get newest papers first.
Choose relevance for focused topic searches; choose date for monitoring recent developments.

RATE LIMITING: arXiv enforces a 3-second minimum between requests. This server handles that automatically.
If you see a rate limit error, wait 60 seconds before retrying — do not call the tool repeatedly in a loop.

TIPS FOR FOUNDATIONAL RESEARCH:
- Use date_to: "2010-12-31" to find classic papers on BDI, SOAR, ACT-R
- Combine with field searches: ti:"BDI" AND abs:"belief desire intention"  
- Try author searches: au:"Rao" AND "BDI" for Anand Rao's foundational BDI work

query.description: Search query using quoted phrases for exact matches (e.g., '"machine learning" OR "deep learning"') or specific technical terms. Avoid overly broad or generic terms.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10, max: 50). Use 15-20 for comprehensive searches.

date_from.description: Start date for papers (YYYY-MM-DD format). Use to find recent work, e.g., '2023-01-01' for last 2 years.

date_to.description: End date for papers (YYYY-MM-DD format). Use with date_from to find historical work, e.g., '2020-12-31' for older research.

categories.description: Strongly recommended: arXiv categories to focus search (e.g., ['cs.AI', 'cs.MA'] for agent research, ['cs.LG'] for ML, ['cs.CL'] for NLP, ['cs.CV'] for vision). Greatly improves relevance.

sort_by.description: Sort results by 'relevance' (most relevant first, default) or 'date' (newest first). Use 'relevance' for focused searches, 'date' for recent developments.

tools.name: download_paper
tools.description: Download a paper from arXiv and return its full text content. Tries the HTML version first for clean extraction; falls back to PDF conversion if HTML is unavailable. Returns the paper content directly so you can read it immediately.

paper_id.description: The arXiv ID of the paper to download (e.g. '2103.12345')

tools.name: list_papers
tools.description: List all papers that have been downloaded and stored locally via download_paper. Returns arXiv IDs only — use read_paper to access content. Returns an empty list if no papers have been downloaded yet. Workflow: search_papers -> download_paper -> list_papers -> read_paper.

tools.name: read_paper
tools.description: Read the full text content of a paper that was previously downloaded via download_paper. Returns the paper in markdown format. Will fail with a clear error if the paper has not been downloaded yet — call download_paper first. Workflow: search_papers -> download_paper -> read_paper.

paper_id.description: The arXiv ID of the paper to read

tools.name: get_abstract
tools.description: Fetch the abstract and metadata of an arXiv paper by ID, WITHOUT downloading the full paper. Use this before download_paper to assess relevance and save tokens. Returns: title, authors, abstract, categories, published date, and PDF URL. Workflow tip: search_papers -> get_abstract (check relevance) -> download_paper (if needed) -> read_paper.

tools.name: semantic_search
tools.description: Semantic similarity search over papers you have already downloaded locally via download_paper. Supports free-text queries (e.g. 'attention mechanisms for long sequences') or finding papers similar to a given paper_id. IMPORTANT: only searches your local downloaded collection — will return empty results if no papers have been downloaded yet. Use search_papers to find papers on arXiv, then download_paper to add them to the local index before using this tool. Requires pro dependencies: uv pip install -e ".[pro]"

paper_id.description: Find papers semantically similar to this arXiv paper ID.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10).

tools.name: reindex
tools.description: Rebuild the local semantic index for downloaded papers.

clear_existing.description: If true, clear the existing index before rebuilding.

tools.name: citation_graph
tools.description: Return papers citing an arXiv paper and papers that it references using Semantic Scholar's citation graph.

tools.name: watch_topic
tools.description: Save or update a persistent research topic watch. When checked via check_alerts, returns only papers published since the last check — acting as a standing alert for new work on a topic. The topic string uses the same query syntax as search_papers (quoted phrases, field specifiers, boolean operators). Examples: '"diffusion models" AND ti:"video generation"', 'au:"LeCun" AND cs.LG'. Calling watch_topic with the same topic string updates the existing watch rather than creating a duplicate. Pair with check_alerts to poll for new papers.

topic.description: Query string to monitor. Uses arXiv search syntax — quoted phrases for exact matches, field specifiers (ti:, au:, abs:), and boolean operators (AND, OR, ANDNOT). Example: '"reinforcement learning" AND "robotics"'.

categories.description: Optional arXiv category filter (e.g. ['cs.LG', 'cs.AI']). Narrows results to specific fields.

max_results.description: Maximum papers to return per alert check (default: 10).

tools.name: check_alerts
tools.description: Check all saved topic watches for newly published papers since the last check. Omitting the topic parameter runs ALL saved watches and returns new papers for each. Passing a topic string checks only that specific watch. Updates each watch's last_checked timestamp after running, so subsequent calls only return newer papers. Use watch_topic to register topics before calling this. Returns a summary with new paper counts and full paper metadata per topic.

topic.description: Optional: check only this specific watched topic (must match the topic string used in watch_topic exactly). Omit to check all saved watches.

marketplace.author: webtools-ai
marketplace.description:
Search for papers on arXiv with advanced filtering and query optimization.

QUERY CONSTRUCTION GUIDELINES:
- Use QUOTED PHRASES for exact matches: "multi-agent systems", "neural networks", "machine learning"
- Combine related concepts with OR: "AI agents" OR "software agents" OR "intelligent agents"  
- Use field-specific searches for precision:
  - ti:"exact title phrase" - search in titles only
  - au:"author name" - search by author
  - abs:"keyword" - search in abstracts only
- Use ANDNOT to exclude unwanted results: "machine learning" ANDNOT "survey"
- For best results, use 2-4 core concepts rather than long keyword lists

ADVANCED SEARCH PATTERNS:
- Field + phrase: ti:"transformer architecture" for papers with exact title phrase
- Multiple fields: au:"Smith" AND ti:"quantum" for author Smith's quantum papers  
- Exclusions: "deep learning" ANDNOT ("survey" OR "review") to exclude survey papers
- Broad + narrow: "artificial intelligence" AND (robotics OR "computer vision")

CATEGORY FILTERING (highly recommended for relevance):
Computer Science:
- cs.AI: Artificial Intelligence
- cs.LG: Machine Learning
- cs.CL: Computation and Language (NLP)
- cs.CV: Computer Vision
- cs.MA: Multi-Agent Systems
- cs.RO: Robotics
- cs.NE: Neural and Evolutionary Computing
- cs.IR: Information Retrieval
- cs.HC: Human-Computer Interaction
- cs.CR: Cryptography and Security
- cs.DB: Databases
Statistics & Math:
- stat.ML: Machine Learning (Statistics)
- stat.AP: Applications
- math.OC: Optimization and Control
- math.ST: Statistics Theory
Physics & Other:
- quant-ph: Quantum Physics
- eess.SP: Signal Processing
- eess.AS: Audio and Speech Processing
- physics.data-an: Data Analysis and Statistics

EXAMPLES OF EFFECTIVE QUERIES:
- ti:"reinforcement learning" with categories: ["cs.LG", "cs.AI"] - for RL papers by title
- au:"Hinton" AND "deep learning" with categories: ["cs.LG"] - for Hinton's deep learning work
- "multi-agent" ANDNOT "survey" with categories: ["cs.MA"] - exclude survey papers
- abs:"transformer" AND ti:"attention" with categories: ["cs.CL"] - attention papers with transformer abstracts

DATE FILTERING: Use YYYY-MM-DD format for historical research:
- date_to: "2015-12-31" - for foundational/classic work (pre-2016)
- date_from: "2020-01-01" - for recent developments (post-2020)
- Both together for specific time periods

RESULT QUALITY: Default sort is RELEVANCE (most pertinent results first). Use sort_by: "date" to get newest papers first.
Choose relevance for focused topic searches; choose date for monitoring recent developments.

RATE LIMITING: arXiv enforces a 3-second minimum between requests. This server handles that automatically.
If you see a rate limit error, wait 60 seconds before retrying — do not call the tool repeatedly in a loop.

TIPS FOR FOUNDATIONAL RESEARCH:
- Use date_to: "2010-12-31" to find classic papers on BDI, SOAR, ACT-R
- Combine with field searches: ti:"BDI" AND abs:"belief desire intention"  
- Try author searches: au:"Rao" AND "BDI" for Anand Rao's foundational BDI work

marketplace.author: toolsmith
marketplace.description: Accesses arXiv papers by ID or search query, returning text and metadata.

tools.name: search_papers
tools.description: Accesses arXiv papers by ID or search query, returning text and metadata.

query.description: Search query using quoted phrases for exact matches (e.g., '"machine learning" OR "deep learning"') or specific technical terms. Avoid overly broad or generic terms.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10, max: 50). Use 15-20 for comprehensive searches.

date_from.description: Start date for papers (YYYY-MM-DD format). Use to find recent work, e.g., '2023-01-01' for last 2 years.

date_to.description: End date for papers (YYYY-MM-DD format). Use with date_from to find historical work, e.g., '2020-12-31' for older research.

categories.description: Strongly recommended: arXiv categories to focus search (e.g., ['cs.AI', 'cs.MA'] for agent research, ['cs.LG'] for ML, ['cs.CL'] for NLP, ['cs.CV'] for vision). Greatly improves relevance.

sort_by.description: Sort results by 'relevance' (most relevant first, default) or 'date' (newest first). Use 'relevance' for focused searches, 'date' for recent developments.

marketplace.author: tavily-ai
marketplace.description: Web search, content extraction, site crawling, site mapping, and deep research powered by Tavily API.

tools.name: tavily_search
tools.description: Search the web for current information on any topic. Use for news, facts, or data beyond your knowledge cutoff. Returns snippets and source URLs.

search_depth.description: The depth of the search. 'basic' for generic results, 'advanced' for more thorough search, 'fast' for optimized low latency with high relevance, 'ultra-fast' for prioritizing latency above all else

topic.description: The category of the search. This will determine which of our agents will be used for the search

time_range.description: The time range back from the current date to include in the search results

start_date.description: Will return all results after the specified start date. Required to be written in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

end_date.description: Will return all results before the specified end date. Required to be written in the format YYYY-MM-DD

max_results.description: The maximum number of search results to return

include_images.description: Include a list of query-related images in the response

include_image_descriptions.description: Include a list of query-related images and their descriptions in the response

include_raw_content.description: Include the cleaned and parsed HTML content of each search result

include_domains.description: A list of domains to specifically include in the search results, if the user asks to search on specific sites set this to the domain of the site

exclude_domains.description: List of domains to specifically exclude, if the user asks to exclude a domain set this to the domain of the site

country.description: Boost search results from a specific country. Must be a full country name (e.g., 'United States', 'Japan', 'Germany'). ISO country codes (e.g., 'us', 'jp') are not supported. Available only if topic is general. See https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/api-reference/search for the full list of supported countries.

include_favicon.description: Whether to include the favicon URL for each result

tools.name: tavily_extract
tools.description: Extract content from URLs. Returns raw page content in markdown or text format.

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from

extract_depth.description: Use 'advanced' for LinkedIn, protected sites, or tables/embedded content

query.description: Query to rerank content chunks by relevance

tools.name: tavily_crawl
tools.description: Crawl a website starting from a URL. Extracts content from pages with configurable depth and breadth.

url.description: The root URL to begin the crawl

max_depth.description: Max depth of the crawl. Defines how far from the base URL the crawler can explore.

max_breadth.description: Max number of links to follow per level of the tree (i.e., per page)

limit.description: Total number of links the crawler will process before stopping

instructions.description: Natural language instructions for the crawler. Instructions specify which types of pages the crawler should return.

select_paths.description: Regex patterns to select only URLs with specific path patterns (e.g., /docs/.*, /api/v1.*)

select_domains.description: Regex patterns to restrict crawling to specific domains or subdomains (e.g., ^docs\.example\.com$)

allow_external.description: Whether to return external links in the final response

extract_depth.description: Advanced extraction retrieves more data, including tables and embedded content, with higher success but may increase latency

format.description: The format of the extracted web page content. markdown returns content in markdown format. text returns plain text and may increase latency.

include_favicon.description: Whether to include the favicon URL for each result

tools.name: tavily_map
tools.description: Map a website's structure. Returns a list of URLs found starting from the base URL.

url.description: The root URL to begin the mapping

max_depth.description: Max depth of the mapping. Defines how far from the base URL the crawler can explore

max_breadth.description: Max number of links to follow per level of the tree (i.e., per page)

limit.description: Total number of links the crawler will process before stopping

instructions.description: Natural language instructions for the crawler

select_paths.description: Regex patterns to select only URLs with specific path patterns (e.g., /docs/.*, /api/v1.*)

select_domains.description: Regex patterns to restrict crawling to specific domains or subdomains (e.g., ^docs\.example\.com$)

allow_external.description: Whether to return external links in the final response

tools.name: tavily_research
tools.description: Perform comprehensive research on a given topic or question. Use this tool when you need to gather information from multiple sources to answer a question or complete a task. Returns a detailed response based on the research findings. Rate limit: 20 requests per minute.

input.description: A comprehensive description of the research task

model.description: Defines the degree of depth of the research. 'mini' is good for narrow tasks with few subtopics. 'pro' is good for broad tasks with many subtopics. 'auto' automatically selects the best model.
